question: What is Antim Sanskar?
answer: Antim Sanskar, literally meaning 'final rite of passage,' is the sixteenth and last of the sixteen Samskaras (sacred life ceremonies) prescribed in the Hindu scriptures. It encompasses all rituals from the moment of death through the cremation and the 13-day mourning period, including the Terahvi ceremony. The Garuda Purana, one of the eighteen Mahapuranas, gives detailed prescriptions for conducting Antim Sanskar with Vedic accuracy.

question: How quickly can you reach us after we call?
answer: We aim to have a representative or pandit reach your family within 30 minutes anywhere within the cities we serve. Our average response time is under 5 minutes for the initial phone call, where we begin immediate coordination. We operate 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year — including all holidays and festivals. In urgent cases, we begin coordination over the phone immediately.

question: Do you serve my community's funeral traditions?
answer: Yes. Our pandits are trained in six major community traditions: Bengali, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, Marathi, and Gujarati — each with their distinct rituals, customs, and scriptural basis. We also serve Bihari, UP, Rajasthani, Odia, and Kashmiri Pandit traditions. When you call us, we will ask about your specific community and tradition to ensure the right pandit is assigned.

question: What is included in your service?
answer: The inclusions depend on the package chosen. Our Mukti package covers Antim Sanskar, cremation coordination, and transport. Moksha adds the first three days of rituals and Asthi Visarjan. Vaikuntha is our comprehensive package covering the full 13-day ritual cycle, two dedicated pandits, live streaming, grief counselling, and legal documentation. All packages are also customizable — please call us to discuss your specific needs.

question: How do you handle NRI families?
answer: We have significant experience with NRI families. If the deceased is in India and the family is abroad, we can manage the entire process on their behalf, with video documentation and live streaming of all ceremonies. If the family needs to fly in, we can arrange freezer box services for body preservation until they arrive. We also handle all legal documentation, NOC certificates, and can arrange intercity transport for the body.

question: Can you arrange Asthi Visarjan at sacred rivers?
answer: Yes, we coordinate Asthi Visarjan at all major sacred tirthas: Haridwar, Varanasi (Manikarnika and Harishchandra Ghats), Prayagraj (Triveni Sangam), Gaya (for Pind Daan), and Rameswaram in the south. We have partnerships with local pandits at each location who are certified to conduct the Visarjan ceremony. We help arrange travel and accommodation for the family as well.

question: Are your pandits formally qualified?
answer: Yes, absolutely. All pandits on our platform hold formal Vedic education — most from recognized institutions such as Sampurnanand Sanskrit University (Varanasi), Kashi Vidwat Parishad, Sanskrit College (Kolkata), Uttaradi Math Vidyalaya (Bangalore), and other accredited Vedic institutions. We conduct background verification, check credentials, and review their knowledge of Antyesti Paddhati before they are approved to serve families through us.

question: What if I don't know which rituals to perform?
answer: This is very common, and we are here for exactly this. When you call us, our pandit will guide your family through everything — what needs to be done, in what order, what it means, and why. You do not need to know any rituals in advance. We explain every step. Many families tell us that this guidance was one of the most comforting parts of the entire process.

question: Can I pre-plan for myself or an elderly parent?
answer: Yes, and we strongly encourage it. Pre-planning is an act of deep love for your family — it removes the burden of decision-making from them during an already difficult time. You can document your wishes, choose your ritual preferences, select which community customs should be observed, and even pre-select a pandit. We maintain secure records and will activate your plan immediately when needed. Contact us to learn more about pre-planning.

question: Do you provide grief counselling?
answer: Yes. We work with a network of certified grief counsellors who understand the Hindu spiritual context of loss and mourning. Grief in the Hindu tradition has a specific framework — the rituals themselves are a form of structured grief — but many families benefit from additional professional support. Grief counselling is included in our Vaikuntha package and can be added to any other package. Sessions are available in Hindi, English, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi, and Gujarati.
